samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">State when rising edge detected.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">State when falling edge detected.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">State variable.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state variable.</reg>
        <reg id="next_rise" type="combinational_logic">Next value for rise output.</reg>
        <reg id="next_down" type="combinational_logic">Next value for down output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the positive edge of the clock or when reset is asserted, the state variable is updated. 
            If the reset signal is low, the state is set to the initial state (state0). 
            If the reset signal is high, the state is updated based on the next_state variable.
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            The next_state variable is determined based on the current state and the input signal a. 
            If the state is state0 and a is 0, the next state remains state0. 
            If a is 1, the next state is state1, and the rise output is set to 1. 
            If the state is state1 and a is 1, the next state remains state1. 
            If a is 0, the next state is state2, and the down output is set to 1. 
            If the state is state2 and a is 0, the next state remains state2. 
            If a is 1, the next state is state1, and the rise output is set to 1.
        </combinational>
        <combinational>
            The next_rise and next_down variables are determined based on the current state. 
            If the state is state0, both outputs are set to 0. 
            If the state is state1, the rise output is set to 0, and the down output is set to 1. 
            If the state is state2, the rise output is set to 1, and the down output is set to 0.
        </combinational>
        <sequential>
            On the negative edge of the clock, the next values for rise and down are assigned to the rise and down output signals, respectively.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
